title: pin livekit to gekko-hetzner-4 for public IP WebRTC
description:
## Summary

- `livekit-server` was pinned to `k3s-3` (home worker, WireGuard IP `192.168.100.4`), which has no reachable public IP for WebRTC ICE — STUN discovers the home router NAT IP but UDP ports 50000–60000 are not forwarded, so media paths fail silently
- Move all four livekit-* Deployments to `gekko-hetzner-4` (`178.104.159.79`), a Hetzner node where STUN correctly discovers the real public IP
- Drop all k3s-3 workarounds: `dnsPolicy: None`, public nameservers (`8.8.8.8`), and `hostAliases` for `livekit-redis`/`livekit-server` — CoreDNS is reachable from Hetzner nodes so none of these are needed

## Post-merge

Run DNS-pin to update `livekit.korczewski.de` and `stream.korczewski.de` to point to the new node:
```bash
task livekit:dns-pin ENV=korczewski APPLY=true
```

## Test plan

- [ ] ArgoCD syncs `workspace-korczewski` cleanly (no SharedResourceWarning)
- [ ] `livekit-server`, `livekit-redis`, `livekit-egress`, `livekit-ingress` pods land on `gekko-hetzner-4`
- [ ] `livekit-server` logs show STUN-discovered IP `178.104.159.79`
- [ ] WebRTC stream connects successfully from a browser on `web.korczewski.de`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
